title: Interview: Discussing the Editing of Sentimental Value with Academy Award Nominee Olivier Bugge Coutté
text: AwardsRadar conducted an exclusive interview with Oscar-nominated editor Olivier Bugge Coutté about his work on Sentimental Value. Coutté discussed his collaborative process with director Joachim Trier, their 30-year friendship, and the creative decisions made during editing. He explained how the film's ambitious temporal structure—spanning a century with multiple perspective shifts—required innovative cutting techniques. Coutté emphasized the importance of emotional truth over technical showiness, stating that the best editing is invisible to the audience. He described specific scenes where he stripped away elements to create powerful moments of silence and understanding between characters. The interview provided insight into the technical tools and creative philosophy that shaped Sentimental Value's narrative presentation.
